SEARCH
Package com.nib.supernaut

Class Task

java.lang.Object
com.nib.supernaut.Task
All Implemented Interfaces:
SecurityContext
public class Task extends Object implements SecurityContext
Represents a Task that is executed in the context of a specific region, terminal, and other associated subsystems. A Task typically processes requests as part of transaction execution within the system.

The Task class encapsulates information such as task status, associated user, terminal, data payload, and program execution logic. It manages the lifecycle of a task, including initialization, execution, and completion, and provides methods to configure and interact with various aspects of the task.

This class supports the execution of transactions by leveraging region-level configurations, authorization mechanisms, terminal handling services, subsystem connections, and error handling.

Responsibilities: - Tracks the execution status of the task. - Handles timeout scenarios during task execution. - Integrates with authentication and authorization providers. - Manages payload data and terminal interactions. - Executes the main program associated with the task's target. - Registers and deregisters the task within its lifecycle in the region. - Manages logging and tracing information for debugging and monitoring.

Task Status: The task goes through different states during its lifecycle: - INIT: The task is initialized but not yet running. - RUNNING: The task is currently executing. - COMPLETED: The task has successfully finished its execution. - TIMEDOUT: The task execution exceeded the configured timeout. - ABORTED: The task terminated prematurely due to an error or another condition.

  • Constructor Details

    • Task

      public Task(SupernautRegion region, PctEntry pct, ITerminalHandler terminal, long taskn, StartCode sc, ITDSubSystem td, ITSSubSystem ts)
      Constructs a Task object with the specified parameters.
      Parameters:
      region - the SupernautRegion to which this task belongs
      pct - the PctEntry object associated with this task
      terminal - the ITerminalHandler responsible for managing terminal-related operations
      taskn - the unique task number identifying this task
      sc - the StartCode object that defines the startup parameters for this task
      td - the ITDSubSystem utilized for TD (Transaction Dispatcher) services
      ts - the ITSSubSystem utilized for TS (Transaction Service) services

  • Method Details

    • data

      public Task data(byte[] data, int datalen)
      Assigns the task data and its length for the task.
      Parameters:
      data - the byte array representing the data to be set
      datalen - the length of the data
      Returns:
      the updated Task object

    • payload

      public Task payload(Object payload)
      Assigns a payload for the task.
      Parameters:
      payload - the payload object to be associated with the task
      Returns:
      the updated Task object

    • qname

      public Task qname(String qname)
      Assigns the start queue name for the task.
      Parameters:
      qname - the queue name to be associated with the task.
      Returns:
      the updated Task object.

    • servlet

      public Task servlet(jakarta.servlet.http.HttpServlet servlet, jakarta.servlet.ServletRequest request, jakarta.servlet.ServletResponse response)
      Assigns the servlet handling the task.
      Parameters:
      servlet - the servlet to be associated with the task.
      request - servlet request.
      response - servlet response.
      Returns:
      the updated Task object.

    • timeout

      public Task timeout(int secs)
      Sets the timeout for the task execution.
      Parameters:
      secs - timeout in seconds.
      Returns:
      the updated Task object.

    • channel

      public Task channel(Channel channel)
      Assigns a channel to the task
      Parameters:
      channel - the channel to be assigned to the task.
      Returns:
      the updated Task object.

    • user

      public Task user(User user)
      Assigns a user to the task.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to be assigned to the task.
      Returns:
      the updated Task object.

    • run

      public CommandResponse run() throws AbendException, OnlineErrorException
      Executes the task defined by the provided parameters and processes all associated operations, including user authentication, terminal handling, authorization checks, and transactional execution. The method also manages resource acquisition and release, logging, and error handling during the task lifecycle.
      Returns:
      a CommandResponse indicating the result of the task execution. Possible outcomes include: - Dfhresp.Transiderr: The PCT entry is null. - Dfhresp.Disabled: The PCT entry is disabled. - Dfhresp.Termiderr: There was an error in terminal handling. - Dfhresp.Notauth: The user is not authorized for the requested PCT entry. - Any response defined by the application logic in case of handled OnlineErrorException. - Success or failure outcome based on task completion.
      Throws:
      AbendException - if an unrecoverable condition occurs during task execution, signaling an abnormal termination.
      OnlineErrorException - if an application-specific error occurs during online transactional processing.

    • isTimedout

      public boolean isTimedout()
      Checks if the task has timed out.
      Returns:

    • getTwalen

      public int getTwalen()
      Returns the transaction work area (TWA) length.
      Returns:
      the TWA length, as obtained from the PCT entry.

    • getTransid

      public String getTransid()
      Retrieves the transaction identifier (EIBTRNID) for the task.
      Returns:
      the transaction identifier associated with this task, as obtained from the PCT entry.

    • getMainProgram

      public String getMainProgram()
      Retrieves the main program name associated with the task.
      Returns:
      the main program name if specified; otherwise, an empty string

    • getTermid

      public String getTermid()
      Retrieves the terminal identifier (EIBTRMID) for the task.
      Returns:
      the terminal identifier associated with this task.

    • setCurrentUser

      public void setCurrentUser(User u)
      Sets the current user for the task.
      Parameters:
      u - the user to be set as the current user.

    • hasPayload

      public boolean hasPayload()
      Checks if the task has a payload associated with it.
      Returns:
      true if the task has a payload, false otherwise.
